description: Length-matched oracle test facts for Q_f ratio R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r}.

facts.context: The quantity R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} for vortex filaments is:
facts.truth: a kernel ratio that cancels stretching
distractors: undefined for vortex configurations
identical to any single-kernel Q_f
restricted to two-dimensional flows

facts.context: Under pure stretching by factor s, the Q_f ratio R_f has fractional variance:
facts.truth: approximately 2%, which is 30x better than standard Q_f
distractors: approximately 60%, identical to the standard Q_f value
significantly worse than what standard Q_f achieves
exactly zero due to perfect cancellation of all terms

facts.context: During Biot-Savart evolution of vortex rings, the Q_f ratio R_f:
distractors: diverges rapidly during evolution
becomes undefined after few steps
performs much worse than standard

facts.context: The combined score (geometric mean of stretch and evolution frac_var) for R_f is:
facts.truth: 0.006, approximately 5x better than any single Q_f variant
distractors: 0.030, approximately equal to the standard Q_f variant
worse than all other tested Q_f variant candidates
undefined because geometric mean diverges for ratios

facts.context: The stretch resistance of R_f = Q_exp / Q_inv arises because:
facts.truth: both grow under stretching at rates that partially cancel
distractors: the ratio stays constant independent of all geometry
exponential and inverse kernels scale identically here
stretching does not alter any Q_f quantity at all

facts.context: The physical interpretation of R_f = Q_{e^(-r)} / Q_{1/r} is:
facts.truth: balance of short-range and all-scale vortex interactions
distractors: total conserved circulation of the vortex configuration
purely kinetic energy content of the vortex filaments
magnetic helicity analog for the hydrodynamic vortices

facts.context: Compared to enstrophy-normalized Q_f / Omega under pure stretching:
facts.truth: R_f achieves 2% frac_var vs 17% for Q_f/Omega
distractors: R_f achieves 25% frac_var vs 5% for Q_f/Omega
both methods give identical fractional variance results
Q_f / Omega is not well-defined for vortex filaments

facts.context: The Q_f ratio concept can be generalized to:
facts.truth: R_{f,g} = Q_f/Q_g for any kernel pair with different scaling
distractors: only the specific exponential and inverse kernel combination
exclusively two-dimensional vortex systems with no extension
no generalization exists beyond the original R_f definition
